--- 
title: Test 
--- 
3. The "Agri & Farmer Identity" Module (কৃষক ও খামারি পরিচিতি)
Since a vast population of Bangladesh is involved in agriculture, tracking this is vital for national food security.

Crop Ledger: What the citizen is currently growing on the land registered in their Assets module.

Smart Procurement: The government can buy paddy/rice directly from the verified farmer's UUDI, cutting out the middlemen (faria/dalal), and sending the money to their Digital Wallet.

Livestock Tagging: Linking cattle or poultry (via ear tags/chips) to the farmer's UUDI for veterinary tracking and insurance.



5. The "Business & Enterprise" Module (বাণিজ্য ও উদ্যোগ)
We briefly mentioned RJSC in the Finance module, but business needs its own space.

Trade License Auto-Renewal: Linked to City Corporation data.

Import/Export (IRC/ERC) Ledger: For businessmen, tracking their port activities.

VAT & Customs Sync: Real-time logging of VAT paid by the business linked to the owner's primary UUDI.



7. The "Consent & Privacy Gateway" (তথ্য সুরক্ষা ও সম্মতি)
This is perhaps the most crucial for a modern democracy. A module dedicated entirely to who is looking at the citizen's data.

Access Logs: The citizen can see every time a bank, a police officer, or a government office queries their UUDI.

Time-Locked Tokens: Instead of giving a photocopy of an ID card, the citizen generates a "10-minute access token" for a specific purpose (e.g., buying a SIM card). After 10 minutes, the telecom company can no longer view the data.

Revoke Rights: The ability to block a specific private entity from seeing their data.
